description_long: This is a weathered, two-story wooden house with a rusted, red metal gable roof and an upper-level balcony supported by wooden posts. The exterior walls are clad in aged, horizontal wooden planks, showing signs of discoloration and wear. The front features three small windows with awnings on the lower level and a wooden door opening onto the balcony above. The balcony is partially enclosed by a simple wooden railing, and a metal downspout runs along the side of the building. The house sits on a slightly raised foundation, with visible support columns and a patchy, stained lower facade. The overall appearance suggests a utilitarian, vernacular style common in older, working-class urban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story, weathered wooden house with a rusted red gable roof, upper balcony, and aged plank siding. The facade features small windows, a wooden door, and a simple railing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, three lower windows with awnings, upper balcony, and wooden door.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the balcony's depth, a large window with an awning, and the metal downspout.
description_view_2: Rear view with a single window and awning, showing the plain wooden siding and roof overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying the gable end, extended downspout, and weathered wall with foundation stains.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door on upper balcony, accessed via steps or platform, with minimal decorative elements and a basic railing.
